title: Sample HS256 JWT payloads issued to service accounts
description: Reference decoded JWT payloads. The encoded token is `<base64url(header)>.<base64url(payload)>.<base64url(signature)>` where the signature is HMAC-SHA256(base64url(header) + '.' + base64url(payload), <secret_material of the kid>). Payload shape matches design.md §8.2.

example_platform_scoped_jwt.note: JWT issued to a platform-plane SA. `tid` claim is ABSENT (not null) because the SA has no tenant scope.
decoded_notes: `sub` is the service_account_id (ULID) — downstream services confirm this against the X-Service-Account-Id header.
`app` = the Application slug (not the ULID) so downstream services can string-compare against config('app.slug').
`tid` is absent — platform-plane SAs have no tenant scope.
`aud` names the intended receiving service; verifiers refuse the token if their config('app.audience') isn't in the array.
`jti` is a fresh UUID per issuance; the audit-log correlation + JWT deny-list are keyed by this value.
TTL: exp - iat = 300 seconds (default from service-accounts.jwt.ttl_seconds).

example_tenant_scoped_jwt.note: JWT issued to a tenant-scoped SA. `tid` claim is populated.
decoded_notes: `tid` = tenant_id (ULID). Downstream services confirm this against the routed tenant context (`X-Tenant-Id` header OR subdomain-resolved tenant).
The kid `app-sports-2026-01` is namespaced with the application slug for operator triage — opt-in via config('service-accounts.kid.include_application_prefix', true).
`aud` names the specific receiving service that processes Braintree webhooks.

example_test_jwt.note: Test JWT issued via `service-account:test-jwt` command or POST /platform/service-accounts/{id}/issue-jwt. Payload shape is identical to the exchange-endpoint issuance; the ServiceAccountJwtIssued event fires with trigger='test' so metrics distinguish this from production traffic.
decoded_notes: TTL: 60 seconds — test JWTs default to a short window to bound blast radius if the token leaks in a screenshot / terminal history.

Otherwise indistinguishable from a production issuance — downstream services process it the same way. The DISTINGUISHING marker is in the ServiceAccountJwtIssued event's `trigger` field, not on the JWT itself.

invariants: `alg` is ALWAYS HS256 in Wave 1a. Wave 1c may introduce RS256 alongside; downstream verifiers select the algorithm from the kid's `algorithm` column, not from the JWT header (JWT-header-alg is checked only for equality with the kid's algorithm to prevent alg-confusion attacks).
`iss` is ALWAYS the identity-service canonical URL. Downstream verifiers reject any other issuer.
`sub` is a service_account_id (ULID `svc_...`), NEVER a user_id (`usr_...`) or platform_user_id (`plu_...`). SA JWTs never impersonate a human.
`app` is present on EVERY SA JWT. Downstream verifiers reject when `app` != their `config('app.slug')`.
`tid` is present ONLY when the SA is tenant-scoped. Presence is exclusive-or with the SA's `tenant_id IS NULL` state — issuance never mixes them.
`jti` is UNIQUE per issuance. Even if a caller replays the same exchange within the same second, a fresh jti is generated.
